items.headline: LeBron James Sets NBA All-Time Regular Season Games Played Record
items.summary: Lakers superstar LeBron James has officially set a new NBA record, playing in his 1,612th regular-season game, surpassing Robert Parish's long-standing mark. This milestone was achieved during the Lakers' narrow 105-104 victory over the Orlando Magic.

items.headline: Key Players on Injury Report: Johnson, Leonard Questionable; Kings' Rookies Sidelined
items.summary: Atlanta Hawks forward Jalen Johnson is questionable for today's game due to a shoulder injury. Kawhi Leonard of the LA Clippers is also listed as questionable with an ankle issue. Additionally, Sacramento Kings rookie Nique Clifford exited their game against the Nets with an ankle injury and will not return, while teammate Killian Hayes suffered a left toe injury.

items.headline: Lakers Edge Magic in Nail-Biter
items.summary: The Los Angeles Lakers secured a dramatic 105-104 victory over the Orlando Magic, with Luke Kennard hitting a clutch game-winning three-pointer. Luka Doncic led the Lakers with 33 points.

items.headline: Knicks Rout Wizards for Sixth Straight Win
items.summary: The New York Knicks dominated the Washington Wizards with a 145-113 win, extending their winning streak to six games. Karl-Anthony Towns posted a double-double with 26 points and 16 rebounds.

items.headline: Thunder Soar Past Wizards Behind SGA's 40
items.summary: The Oklahoma City Thunder continued their impressive season with a 132-111 dispatching of the Washington Wizards. MVP frontrunner Shai Gilgeous-Alexander exploded for 40 points.

items.headline: Cavs Hold Off Pelicans
items.summary: The Cleveland Cavaliers defeated the New Orleans Pelicans 111-106. Donovan Mitchell led the scoring for the Cavaliers with 27 points.

items.headline: Hornets Dismantle Grizzlies with Shooting Clinic
items.summary: Charlotte put on a dominant display, beating the Memphis Grizzlies 124-101. LaMelo Ball led the charge with 29 points and seven three-pointers.

items.headline: Jokic Triple-Double Powers Nuggets Past Blazers
items.summary: Nikola Jokic recorded his 35th triple-double of the season with 22 points, 14 rebounds, and 14 assists as the Denver Nuggets beat the Portland Trail Blazers 128-112.

items.headline: Kings Outlast Nets in Tight Contest
items.summary: The Sacramento Kings held off the Brooklyn Nets 126-122. Malik Monk erupted for 32 points, including 10 in the final five minutes, and hit seven three-pointers.

items.headline: Suns Take Down Raptors
items.summary: The Phoenix Suns defeated the Toronto Raptors with a final score of 120-98.

items.headline: Timberwolves Secure Rare Win in Boston
items.summary: The Minnesota Timberwolves earned a 102-92 victory over the Boston Celtics, their first win in Boston since 2005. Bones Hyland led the team with 23 points.

items.headline: Jokic's Dominance Continues with 35th Triple-Double
items.statContext: Nikola Jokic (DEN) recorded his 35th triple-double of the season against Portland.

items.headline: Shai Gilgeous-Alexander Drops 40 Points
items.statContext: Shai Gilgeous-Alexander (OKC) led the Thunder with an explosive 40-point performance against the Wizards.

items.headline: Luka Doncic's All-Around Masterclass
items.statContext: Luka Doncic (LAL) was the driving force for the Lakers in their narrow win over the Magic.

items.headline: Malik Monk Catches Fire for Kings
items.statContext: Malik Monk (SAC) scored 32 points, including 10 in the final five minutes, hitting seven three-pointers against the Nets.

items.headline: Deni Avdija Shines for Trail Blazers
items.statContext: Israeli forward Deni Avdija (POR) led the Trail Blazers in scoring and assists in their game against the Nuggets.

items.headline: LaMelo Ball Leads Hornets' Offensive Barrage
items.statContext: LaMelo Ball (CHA) orchestrated the offense beautifully, hitting seven three-pointers in a high-scoring effort.

items.headline: Isaiah Hartenstein's Near Triple-Double
items.statContext: Isaiah Hartenstein (OKC) had a massive double-double with 20 rebounds and 10 assists, nearly achieving a triple-double.

items.headline: Karl-Anthony Towns Dominates Boards for Knicks
items.statContext: Karl-Anthony Towns (NYK) posted a strong double-double in the Knicks' victory over the Wizards.

items.headline: Today's Marquee Matchups: Lakers Face Pistons, Jazz Host Raptors
items.summary: NBA action continues tonight with several intriguing matchups. The Los Angeles Lakers are scheduled to visit the Detroit Pistons. Elsewhere, the Utah Jazz will host the Toronto Raptors, and the Orlando Magic will take on the Indiana Pacers.

items.headline: Playoff Picture: Pistons Lead East, Thunder Top West
items.summary: As the season progresses, the Detroit Pistons currently hold the top seed in the Eastern Conference with a 51-19 record. In the Western Conference, the Oklahoma City Thunder maintain their lead as the top seed.

items.headline: NBA Legends Isiah Thomas, DeMarcus Cousins Headed to Delhi
items.summary: NBA legends Isiah Thomas and DeMarcus Cousins are set to headline a fan event in Delhi during the 2026 NBA Playoffs, offering Indian fans a unique opportunity to interact with global basketball icons.
